title: lichen_l2_send returns 0 unconditionally on success - missing packet unref
description:
File: firmware/bridge-zephyr/subsys/lichen/lichen_l2.c:97-182

lichen_l2_send() returns 0 on success but does NOT call net_pkt_unref(pkt). 

Per Zephyr net_l2 contract: if send returns 0, L2 took ownership and must free the packet. The caller (IPv6 stack) will not free it.

Trigger: Any successful TX.

Bad behavior: Memory leak of net_pkt structures. Eventually exhausts the packet pool, causing net_pkt_rx_alloc_with_buffer failures and dropped packets.

Fix: Add net_pkt_unref(pkt) before returning 0 in success path (line 181).
